title: Prompt Caching In Agents | EARENDIL
excerpt: How prompt caching shapes the cost, latency, tools, and architecture of coding agents, and what Pi does to keep cache behavior visible.
content_html:
Large language models are often thought of like functions: send in some text, receive some text. That is a useful abstraction, but it ignores one of the most important parts of running a coding agent: most of the input is the same as last time. In other words we mostly append to it.

A coding agent sends the model its system prompt, tool definitions, project instructions, conversation history, tool calls, and tool results. On the next turn it sends almost all of that again, plus a small amount of new material. Once a session has grown to tens or hundreds of thousands of tokens, recomputing the whole prompt for every turn is slow and expensive.

Prompt caching is what makes this somewhat economic, but it is also quite fragile. A changed tool definition, a model switch or a provider routing decision can turn what one would expect to be a cheap incremental request into a full replay of the context.

For coding agents, cache behavior is therefore not just an implementation detail or optimization. It affects latency, cost, tool design, session design, and even which product features should be made available.

What a KV Cache Contains

A transformer processes a prompt in two broad phases. During prefill, it reads the input tokens and computes attention state for them. During decode, it produces new tokens one at a time.

At each attention layer, every processed token produces a key and a value. These are not quite like key-value lookups in a hash table: both are arrays of numbers, usually floats or lower-precision quantized values. When processing a new token, the model compares that token's query with the earlier keys to determine how relevant each earlier token is. It then uses those relevance scores to form a weighted mixture of the corresponding values. In that sense, a key is what the model matches against, while a value is the information it retrieves (but the lookup is fuzzy rather than "returning a single exact match" like a dictionary lookup.)

Those keys and values are retained so that the next generated token can attend to everything that came before without recomputing the earlier tokens. This retained state is the KV cache.

Conceptually, a request looks like this:

request 1:

[system][tools][user][assistant][tool result][user]
<--------------------- prefill -------------------->
                       |
                       K and V tensors per token and layer

request 2:

[system][tools][user][assistant][tool result][user][new]
<---------------- reusable prefix ----------------><--->
                                                    |
                                                    new work

The real representations are more complicated, model-specific, and "quite" large. The important property is that they correspond to a particular token prefix. Two prompts that mean the same thing but tokenize differently do not share a KV cache. If a token changes in the middle, everything after that token is a different continuation.

Prompt caching extends the lifetime of this state beyond one generation. When the next API request from the coding agent begins with the same tokens, the inference system can reuse the stored work for the matching prefix and prefill only the new suffix. So far, the theory.

Where the Cache Lives

In order for a cache to work it needs to be stored somewhere, and it needs to be addressable. There are two broad ways inference systems make KV caches available to a later request.

The simpler approach is session affinity. It works by keeping the KV cache on or near the GPU that computed it, and routing the next request back to the same worker. A session ID or prompt-cache key becomes a trivial routing hint and so you can potentially even deal with this problem on the HTTP load balancer level without having to look into the payload.

request(session-42) --> router --> worker 7 --> GPU 7 KV cache
next(session-42)    --> router --> worker 7 --> GPU 7 KV cache

This avoids moving a very large cache over the network. It is fast when it works, but it constrains scheduling. The selected worker can become overloaded, restart, or evict the entry. A router may also decide that balancing the fleet is more important than preserving one session's cache. It is however a very attractive solution because it works with little extra deployed infrastructure and hardware.

The other approach is to distribute the cache. KV blocks can be stored in another memory tier or made available across workers, so a request is not tied as tightly to one GPU.

                         +--------------------+
request --> scheduler -->| worker 3 / GPU 3   |
             |           +--------------------+
             |
             +----------> distributed KV blocks
             |
             +----------> worker 9 / GPU 9

That improves scheduling flexibility and recovery, but moving, indexing, and retaining KV blocks is itself a systems problem. Implementations mix GPU memory, host memory, local storage, remote storage, prefix-aware routing, and eviction policies in different ways.

To put KV caches into perspective: they can be large but they are in some ways smaller than one would assume. With various tricks, the size of KV caches can be reduced to a handful of gigabytes, even for long conversations.

Caches and Prefixes

Pi sessions are trees, not lists. /tree can move the active conversation back to an earlier point and continue along another branch. A rewind can discard the active suffix without deleting it from the session file. A new branch can share most of the old context, a little of it, or effectively none of it. This design is not unique to Pi, quite a few coding agents have something at least conceptually similar. Even if you do not represent the session as a tree, it's not uncommon for agents to have some form of rewinding.

                             +-- E -- F  another branch
                             |
session S: root -- A -- B -- C -- D  current branch
                   |
                   +-- Z  branch near the start

All three branches can have the same Pi session ID. From the router's perspective they are one session. From the prompt cache's perspective they are three token sequences with only partial prefix overlap.

If the cache keeps reusable prefix blocks, jumping from D to F may still reuse root -> C. If it only retains the hottest continuation, if the shared blocks were evicted, or if the request is routed elsewhere, the hit can be much smaller. Jumping to Z may preserve only the system prompt and initial tool definitions even though it starts from A. The precise cache management behavior here depends greatly on the providers.

The reverse can also happen. /fork or a new session can produce a new session ID while carrying over a large amount of identical context. A routing system that isolates caches by session key may fail to notice that useful overlap.

The reusable prefix determines what work can be cached. Session identity merely helps the infrastructure find likely content. On some systems the routing key is crucial to manage caches, on others it's merely an optimization.

Explicit vs Automatic Prefix Caching

Provider APIs expose caching in two main styles.

Anthropic's traditional interface uses explicit cache_control points. The client marks boundaries after stable parts of the request, such as the system prompt, tool definitions, or the latest cacheable conversation content. The server can then write or look up the prefix ending at those points. The boundary is explicit, but reuse still requires the content before it to match. Not only are the cache points explicit, so is the pricing. You pay for cache writes, and you get to choose for how long which comes at different price points.

Other APIs use automatic prefix caching. The client sends the request normally, and the provider finds a reusable prefix without client-placed breakpoints. A prompt-cache key or session header may improve routing or grouping, but it does not make different prefixes equal.

Why Tool Loadouts Trash Caches

Tool definitions usually appear before the conversation and they are "folded" into the system prompt internally. Their names, descriptions, and JSON schemas are model input just like any other text. Adding one tool, removing one, changing its schema, or even serializing the tools in a different order can move the first mismatch close to the start of the prompt.

turn 1: [system][read][write][bash][conversation...........]
turn 2: [system][read][write][bash][deploy][conversation...]
                                   |
                                   old conversation is now
                                   after a mismatch

This is a common surprise with plugin systems and MCP-style tool catalogs. Loading a tool only when it becomes relevant sounds efficient because fewer schemas are sent initially. On most models, however, the newly expanded loadout invalidates the cached conversation that follows it. Saving a few tool-schema tokens can cause tens of thousands of conversation tokens to be processed again.

Some newer model APIs support additive tool loading. A tool can become available at a specific tool result inside the transcript instead of being inserted into the original tool list. The old prefix remains unchanged:

[system][initial tools][conversation][new tool][next turn]
<--------- cached prefix ----------->

Pi nowadays supports this for models with native deferred-tool mechanisms. When an extension makes a purely additive change with setActiveTools(), Pi records the added names on the tool result. For supported Anthropic models it uses deferred definitions and a tool_reference and for supported OpenAI models it emits the corresponding tool-search items. Other models get a safe fallback: Pi sends the complete active tool list on the next request, which works functionally but may wipe the prompt cache.

The word additive matters as removing tools, replacing one loadout with another, or changing prompt snippets still changes earlier input. An extension that rebuilds the system prompt, shuffles tool order, injects timestamps, or changes active tools every turn can accidentally defeat caching for the entire session.

Extensibility means Pi cannot guarantee cache stability on behalf of every extension. We can provide cache-friendly mechanisms; extensions still have to use them and from what we have seen, for many extensions cache efficiency is an afterthought. This is, in part, because when you pay on a fixed subscription the associated cost with cache misses is not quite as obvious.

Interruptions and TTLs

Some important prompt caches have short default lifetimes. Anthropic's default five-minute cache is particularly important because it is shorter than many normal coding activities. If you go to sip a coffee when using Fable, and you come back 10 minutes later, a single "say hi" message will cost you more money than you expect.

That's because while the user may think of a coding session as continuously active, the inference provider sees a sequence of isolated requests:

model request --> run tests for 7 minutes --> model request
                  no cache traffic here

A long build, a test suite, lunch, a meeting, or simply stopping to review a diff can outlive the cache. The next request contains the same prompt, but the stored KV state is gone and the prefix is billed again as input.

Since Pi is currently not a permitted harness on Anthropic's subscription we're following the 5 minute default that Anthropic recommends for API users. However from looking at Claude Code's codebase we know that for their own subscription users, they are increasing that cache timeout to one hour. The increased cost of this however often is not worth it, when you need to pay API token prices.

But you can opt into this. Some providers such as Anthropic expose longer retention controls. For supported direct APIs, Pi users can set PI_CACHE_RETENTION=long to request them. That is still only a request: Pi cannot force a gateway to retain an entry, prevent eviction under memory pressure, or keep a cache alive while no model request is being made.

The Price of a Miss

Providers usually price uncached input, cache writes, and cache reads differently. Cache reads are commonly discounted because the expensive prefill work has already happened. Cache writes can carry a premium because the provider is promising to retain state for later use.

Imagine a coding session with 100,000 tokens of history followed by a short new request as the Fable example above. When the cache works, almost all of that history is charged at the lower cache-read price. Only the small amount of new material needs to be processed at the regular input price and potentially written to the cache.

When the cache misses, the provider has to process the entire 100,000-token history again at the regular input price. It may also charge to write that history back into the cache. This is why a short request such as continue can be surprisingly expensive after a cache expires. In a long coding session, re-reading the old input can cost much more than generating the next answer.

Caching also has a chance to create non-obvious incentives.

The user should want high hit rates because they reduce latency and price. An inference operator that owns the GPUs should want them too: less prefill work means more requests served with the same hardware. A well-designed cached-token discount can align both sides while leaving the operator with better margins.

A gateway or reseller can have a different incentive. If it earns revenue from input tokens billed at the uncached rate, a cache miss can produce a larger customer invoice than a hit. Whether that also produces more profit depends on its upstream costs, contracts, and who operates the cache. In a badly aligned stack, the party responsible for routing may not bear the full cost of misses, while the party billing the user earns more revenue when they happen.

That does not mean providers sabotage caches but it means cache performance should be observable. Users should not have to infer that only from a surprisingly large bill. Understanding if something odd is going on with caches can be an important insight.

Strict cache adherence also means less flexibility for a gateway to route you to the best option in-between turns. You might want to take a cache hit to continue with a different model which from that point onwards might be more economical, or it might be the case that you might be better off load balancing to another provider.

Why Pi Does Not Prune Aggressively

Now that you've made it this far, you probably have an idea why Pi does not prune tool calls. It is tempting to control cost by continuously deleting old tool results or rewriting history and sometimes that is necessary, especially near the context-window limit. But as we have learned, pruning has a cache cost of its own.

Deleting content from the middle changes the prefix at the deletion point. All surviving conversation after it may need to be processed again. The immediate cost of rewriting a long cached context can exceed the future savings from removing a small number of cheaply cached tokens.

A rough break-even comparison is:

one-time rewrite cost
    ~= surviving tokens after the edit * (uncached price - cache-read price)

future savings per turn
    ~= pruned tokens * cache-read price

This is not only an accounting question as old tool results often contain the evidence the model used to make later decisions. Removing them can degrade behavior even if a summary preserves the gist.

Pi therefore prefers a stable, append-oriented transcript and does not treat every old token as waste. Compaction is available when context pressure justifies a lossy rewrite. Because compaction deliberately creates new context rather than accidentally re-billing an unchanged prompt, Pi treats it as a cache reset rather than a cache failure in its session statistics.

The goal is not the smallest possible prompt but the best trade-off among model context, cache reuse, latency, and price.

Simultaneously there can be a case for pruning too. If you are working with providers that do not discount you for good cache usage, or it's for whatever reason not possible to get high cache rates, it might be preferable to prune. It definitely improves the opportunity for the router to balance between different backends as caches are not transferable.

What Pi Can and Cannot Do

Pi works to keep stable inputs stable. It passes a consistent session ID and provider-specific cache hints, places explicit cache points for APIs that require them, records cache-read and cache-write usage, and supports message-anchored additive tool loading where models allow it. Its default transcript behavior also avoids gratuitously rewriting old context.

Pi cannot control every layer after the request leaves the machine. It cannot choose a provider's eviction policy, extend a cache beyond what the API permits, keep a particular GPU alive, or guarantee that a gateway honors affinity. It also cannot preserve a prefix that an extension changes.

What it can do is make cache health visible.

The interactive footer shows cumulative cache reads and writes as R and W, plus CH for the latest request's cache-hit rate. The /session command gives a fuller view: total cached and uncached input, cumulative hit rate, cost, and an estimate of tokens and dollars re-billed by significant cache misses.

Messages
Total: 178
User: 6
Assistant: 58
Tools: 114 calls, 114 results

Tokens
Input: 7,129,883
  Cached: 6,776,832 (95.0%)
  Uncached: 353,051
Output: 30,013
Total: 7,159,896

Cost
Total: $6.054
Cache Re-billed: $0.728 (161,744 tokens, 2 misses)

Users who want misses called out as they happen can enable Show cache miss notices in /settings, corresponding to showCacheMissNotices in settings.json. Pi then inserts a warning after a significant miss, including the estimated re-billed tokens and cost. When it can observe a model switch or an idle gap beyond the usual short TTL, it says so. For other misses it reports the fact without pretending to know what happened inside the provider.

Common Reasons for Worse Cache Performance

When a session's cache-hit rate looks wrong, the usual causes are:

  1. Idling. A command, review, or conversation pause exceeds the provider's retention window.
  2. Model or provider switches. KV state is model-specific and generally does not move across providers.
  3. Branch navigation. /tree, rewinds, forks, and alternate branches can change the active token sequence even when the session ID remains the same.
  4. Compaction or manual history rewriting. These intentionally replace part of the prompt and establish a new prefix.
  5. Tool and reasoning level changes. Adding, removing, reordering, or editing tool definitions changes an early part of the request unless the model supports message-anchored loading and the change is purely additive. Reasoning level changes usually have the same effect.
  6. Dynamic system prompts. Timestamps, random values, changing project context, and extension-provided prompt snippets can invalidate everything after them.
  7. Extension context transforms. An extension that modifies old messages or provider payloads can make an apparently stable Pi transcript unstable on the wire.
  8. Provider routing and eviction. The prompt can be identical and still miss because the relevant KV blocks are no longer available where the request lands.

Common Reasons for Worse Cache Performance When a session's cache-hit rate looks wrong, the usual causes are: Idling. A command, review, or conversation pause exceeds the provider's retention window. Model or provider switches. KV state is model-specific and generally does not move across providers. Branch navigation. /tree, rewinds, forks, and alternate branches can change the active token sequence even when the session ID remains the same. Compaction or manual history rewriting. These intentionally replace part of the prompt and establish a new prefix. Tool and reasoning level changes. Adding, removing, reordering, or editing tool definitions changes an early part of the request unless the model supports message-anchored loading and the change is purely additive. Reasoning level changes usually have the same effect. Dynamic system prompts. Timestamps, random values, changing project context, and extension-provided prompt snippets can invalidate everything after them. Extension context transforms. An extension that modifies old messages or provider payloads can make an apparently stable Pi transcript unstable on the wire. Provider routing and eviction. The prompt can be identical and still miss because the relevant KV blocks are no longer available where the request lands.

blocks.html_en: Large language models are often thought of like functions: send in some text, receive some text. That is a useful abstraction, but it ignores one of the most important parts of running a coding agent: most of the input is the same as last time. In other words we mostly append to it.

sentences.en: Large language models are often thought of like functions: send in some text, receive some text.
sentences.zh: 大型语言模型通常被认为是类似的函数：发送一些文本，接收一些文本。

sentences.en: That is a useful abstraction, but it ignores one of the most important parts of running a coding agent: most of the input is the same as last time.
sentences.zh: 这是一个有用的抽象，但它忽略了运行编码代理的最重要部分之一：大部分输入与上次相同。

sentences.en: In other words we mostly append to it.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，我们主要是附加到它上面。

blocks.html_en: A coding agent sends the model its system prompt, tool definitions, project instructions, conversation history, tool calls, and tool results. On the next turn it sends almost all of that again, plus a small amount of new material. Once a session has grown to tens or hundreds of thousands of tokens, recomputing the whole prompt for every turn is slow and expensive.

sentences.en: A coding agent sends the model its system prompt, tool definitions, project instructions, conversation history, tool calls, and tool results.
sentences.zh: 编码代理向模型发送其系统提示、工具定义、项目说明、对话历史记录、工具调用和工具结果。

sentences.en: On the next turn it sends almost all of that again, plus a small amount of new material.
sentences.zh: 在下一个回合中，它会再次发送几乎所有内容，以及少量新材料。

sentences.en: Once a session has grown to tens or hundreds of thousands of tokens, recomputing the whole prompt for every turn is slow and expensive.
sentences.zh: 一旦会话增长到数万或数十万个令牌，为每个回合重新计算整个提示既缓慢又昂贵。

blocks.html_en: Prompt caching is what makes this somewhat economic, but it is also quite fragile. A changed tool definition, a model switch or a provider routing decision can turn what one would expect to be a cheap incremental request into a full replay of the context.

sentences.en: Prompt caching is what makes this somewhat economic, but it is also quite fragile.
sentences.zh: 即时缓存使得这有点经济，但它也相当脆弱。

sentences.en: A changed tool definition, a model switch or a provider routing decision can turn what one would expect to be a cheap incremental request into a full replay of the context.
sentences.zh: 更改的工具定义、模型切换或提供者路由决策可以将人们期望的廉价增量请求转变为上下文的完整重播。

blocks.html_en: For coding agents, cache behavior is therefore not just an implementation detail or optimization. It affects latency, cost, tool design, session design, and even which product features should be made available.

sentences.en: For coding agents, cache behavior is therefore not just an implementation detail or optimization.
sentences.zh: 因此，对于编码代理来说，缓存行为不仅仅是实现细节或优化。

sentences.en: It affects latency, cost, tool design, session design, and even which product features should be made available.
sentences.zh: 它会影响延迟、成本、工具设计、会话设计，甚至应该提供哪些产品功能。

blocks.html_en: What a KV Cache Contains

sentences.en: What a KV Cache Contains
sentences.zh: KV缓存包含什么

blocks.html_en: A transformer processes a prompt in two broad phases. During prefill, it reads the input tokens and computes attention state for them. During decode, it produces new tokens one at a time.

sentences.en: A transformer processes a prompt in two broad phases.
sentences.zh: 变压器分两个主要阶段处理提示。

sentences.en: During prefill, it reads the input tokens and computes attention state for them.
sentences.zh: 在预填充期间，它读取输入标记并计算它们的注意力状态。

sentences.en: During decode, it produces new tokens one at a time.
sentences.zh: 在解码过程中，它一次产生一个新的令牌。

blocks.html_en: At each attention layer, every processed token produces a key and a value. These are not quite like key-value lookups in a hash table: both are arrays of numbers, usually floats or lower-precision quantized values. When processing a new token, the model compares that token's query with the earlier keys to determine how relevant each earlier token is. It then uses those relevance scores to form a weighted mixture of the corresponding values. In that sense, a key is what the model matches against, while a value is the information it retrieves (but the lookup is fuzzy rather than "returning a single exact match" like a dictionary lookup.)

sentences.en: At each attention layer, every processed token produces a key and a value.
sentences.zh: 在每个注意力层，每个处理的令牌都会生成一个键和一个值。

sentences.en: These are not quite like key-value lookups in a hash table: both are arrays of numbers, usually floats or lower-precision quantized values.
sentences.zh: 这些与哈希表中的键值查找不太相似：两者都是数字数组，通常是浮点数或较低精度的量化值。

sentences.en: When processing a new token, the model compares that token's query with the earlier keys to determine how relevant each earlier token is.
sentences.zh: 处理新令牌时，模型会将该令牌的查询与早期的键进行比较，以确定每个早期令牌的相关程度。

sentences.en: It then uses those relevance scores to form a weighted mixture of the corresponding values.
sentences.zh: 然后，它使用这些相关性分数来形成相应值的加权混合。

sentences.en: In that sense, a key is what the model matches against, while a value is the information it retrieves (but the lookup is fuzzy rather than "returning a single exact match" like a dictionary lookup.)
sentences.zh: 从这个意义上说，键是模型匹配的内容，而值是它检索的信息（但查找是模糊的，而不是像字典查找那样“返回单个精确匹配”。）

blocks.html_en: Those keys and values are retained so that the next generated token can attend to everything that came before without recomputing the earlier tokens. This retained state is the KV cache.

sentences.en: Those keys and values are retained so that the next generated token can attend to everything that came before without recomputing the earlier tokens.
sentences.zh: 这些键和值将被保留，以便下一个生成的令牌可以处理之前发生的所有事情，而无需重新计算早期的令牌。

sentences.en: This retained state is the KV cache.
sentences.zh: 这个保留的状态就是KV缓存。

blocks.html_en: Conceptually, a request looks like this:

sentences.en: Conceptually, a request looks like this:
sentences.zh: 从概念上讲，请求如下所示：

blocks.html_en: The real representations are more complicated, model-specific, and "quite" large. The important property is that they correspond to a particular token prefix. Two prompts that mean the same thing but tokenize differently do not share a KV cache. If a token changes in the middle, everything after that token is a different continuation.

sentences.en: The real representations are more complicated, model-specific, and "quite" large.
sentences.zh: 真实的表示更加复杂，特定于模型，并且“相当”大。

sentences.en: The important property is that they correspond to a particular token prefix.
sentences.zh: 重要的属性是它们对应于特定的令牌前缀。

sentences.en: Two prompts that mean the same thing but tokenize differently do not share a KV cache.
sentences.zh: 两个含义相同但标记不同的提示不共享 KV 缓存。

sentences.en: If a token changes in the middle, everything after that token is a different continuation.
sentences.zh: 如果令牌在中间发生变化，则该令牌之后的所有内容都是不同的延续。

blocks.html_en: Prompt caching extends the lifetime of this state beyond one generation. When the next API request from the coding agent begins with the same tokens, the inference system can reuse the stored work for the matching prefix and prefill only the new suffix. So far, the theory.

sentences.en: Prompt caching extends the lifetime of this state beyond one generation.
sentences.zh: 即时缓存将这种状态的生命周期延长了一代以上。

sentences.en: When the next API request from the coding agent begins with the same tokens, the inference system can reuse the stored work for the matching prefix and prefill only the new suffix.
sentences.zh: 当来自编码代理的下一个 API 请求以相同的标记开始时，推理系统可以重用匹配前缀的存储工作，并仅预填充新的后缀。

sentences.en: So far, the theory.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，理论。

blocks.html_en: Where the Cache Lives

sentences.en: Where the Cache Lives
sentences.zh: 缓存所在的位置

blocks.html_en: In order for a cache to work it needs to be stored somewhere, and it needs to be addressable. There are two broad ways inference systems make KV caches available to a later request.

sentences.en: In order for a cache to work it needs to be stored somewhere, and it needs to be addressable.
sentences.zh: 为了使缓存发挥作用，它需要存储在某个地方，并且需要可寻址。

sentences.en: There are two broad ways inference systems make KV caches available to a later request.
sentences.zh: 推理系统有两种主要方式使 KV 缓存可供以后的请求使用。

blocks.html_en: The simpler approach is session affinity. It works by keeping the KV cache on or near the GPU that computed it, and routing the next request back to the same worker. A session ID or prompt-cache key becomes a trivial routing hint and so you can potentially even deal with this problem on the HTTP load balancer level without having to look into the payload.

sentences.en: The simpler approach is session affinity.
sentences.zh: 更简单的方法是会话关联性。

sentences.en: It works by keeping the KV cache on or near the GPU that computed it, and routing the next request back to the same worker.
sentences.zh: 它的工作原理是将 KV 缓存保留在计算它的 GPU 上或附近，并将下一个请求路由回同一个工作线程。

sentences.en: A session ID or prompt-cache key becomes a trivial routing hint and so you can potentially even deal with this problem on the HTTP load balancer level without having to look into the payload.
sentences.zh: 会话 ID 或提示缓存键成为一个简单的路由提示，因此您甚至可以在 HTTP 负载均衡器级别上处理此问题，而无需查看有效负载。

blocks.html_en: This avoids moving a very large cache over the network. It is fast when it works, but it constrains scheduling. The selected worker can become overloaded, restart, or evict the entry. A router may also decide that balancing the fleet is more important than preserving one session's cache. It is however a very attractive solution because it works with little extra deployed infrastructure and hardware.

sentences.en: This avoids moving a very large cache over the network.
sentences.zh: 这避免了通过网络移动非常大的缓存。

sentences.en: It is fast when it works, but it constrains scheduling.
sentences.zh: 它工作时速度很快，但它限制了调度。

sentences.en: The selected worker can become overloaded, restart, or evict the entry.
sentences.zh: 选定的工作线程可能会过载、重新启动或驱逐该条目。

sentences.en: A router may also decide that balancing the fleet is more important than preserving one session's cache.
sentences.zh: 路由器还可能认为平衡队列比保留一个会话的缓存更重要。

sentences.en: It is however a very attractive solution because it works with little extra deployed infrastructure and hardware.
sentences.zh: 然而，它是一个非常有吸引力的解决方案，因为它几乎不需要额外部署的基础设施和硬件。

blocks.html_en: The other approach is to distribute the cache. KV blocks can be stored in another memory tier or made available across workers, so a request is not tied as tightly to one GPU.

sentences.en: The other approach is to distribute the cache.
sentences.zh: 另一种方法是分布式缓存。

sentences.en: KV blocks can be stored in another memory tier or made available across workers, so a request is not tied as tightly to one GPU.
sentences.zh: KV 块可以存储在另一内存层中，也可以跨工作线程使用，因此请求不会与一个 GPU 紧密联系。

blocks.html_en: That improves scheduling flexibility and recovery, but moving, indexing, and retaining KV blocks is itself a systems problem. Implementations mix GPU memory, host memory, local storage, remote storage, prefix-aware routing, and eviction policies in different ways.

sentences.en: That improves scheduling flexibility and recovery, but moving, indexing, and retaining KV blocks is itself a systems problem.
sentences.zh: 这提高了调度灵活性和恢复能力，但是移动、索引和保留 KV 块本身就是一个系统问题。

sentences.en: Implementations mix GPU memory, host memory, local storage, remote storage, prefix-aware routing, and eviction policies in different ways.
sentences.zh: 实现以不同方式混合 GPU 内存、主机内存、本地存储、远程存储、前缀感知路由和逐出策略。

blocks.html_en: To put KV caches into perspective: they can be large but they are in some ways smaller than one would assume. With various tricks, the size of KV caches can be reduced to a handful of gigabytes, even for long conversations.

sentences.en: To put KV caches into perspective: they can be large but they are in some ways smaller than one would assume.
sentences.zh: 从角度来看 KV 缓存：它们可能很大，但在某些方面比人们想象的要小。

sentences.en: With various tricks, the size of KV caches can be reduced to a handful of gigabytes, even for long conversations.
sentences.zh: 通过各种技巧，KV 缓存的大小可以减少到几 GB，即使对于长时间的对话也是如此。

blocks.html_en: Caches and Prefixes

sentences.en: Caches and Prefixes
sentences.zh: 缓存和前缀

blocks.html_en: Pi sessions are trees, not lists. /tree can move the active conversation back to an earlier point and continue along another branch. A rewind can discard the active suffix without deleting it from the session file. A new branch can share most of the old context, a little of it, or effectively none of it. This design is not unique to Pi, quite a few coding agents have something at least conceptually similar. Even if you do not represent the session as a tree, it's not uncommon for agents to have some form of rewinding.

sentences.en: Pi sessions are trees, not lists. /tree can move the active conversation back to an earlier point and continue along another branch.
sentences.zh: Pi 会话是树，而不是列表。 /tree 可以将活动对话移回较早的点并沿着另一个分支继续。

sentences.en: A rewind can discard the active suffix without deleting it from the session file.
sentences.zh: 倒回可以丢弃活动后缀，而不将其从会话文件中删除。

sentences.en: A new branch can share most of the old context, a little of it, or effectively none of it.
sentences.zh: 新分支可以共享大部分旧上下文，也可以共享一小部分，或者实际上完全不共享。

sentences.en: This design is not unique to Pi, quite a few coding agents have something at least conceptually similar.
sentences.zh: 这种设计并不是 Pi 独有的，很多编码代理至少在概念上都有类似的东西。

sentences.en: Even if you do not represent the session as a tree, it's not uncommon for agents to have some form of rewinding.
sentences.zh: 即使您不将会话表示为树，代理进行某种形式的倒带也并不罕见。

blocks.html_en: All three branches can have the same Pi session ID. From the router's perspective they are one session. From the prompt cache's perspective they are three token sequences with only partial prefix overlap.

sentences.en: All three branches can have the same Pi session ID.
sentences.zh: 所有三个分支可以具有相同的 Pi 会话 ID。

sentences.en: From the router's perspective they are one session.
sentences.zh: 从路由器的角度来看，它们是一个会话。

sentences.en: From the prompt cache's perspective they are three token sequences with only partial prefix overlap.
sentences.zh: 从提示缓存的角度来看，它们是三个标记序列，仅部分前缀重叠。

blocks.html_en: If the cache keeps reusable prefix blocks, jumping from D to F may still reuse root -> C. If it only retains the hottest continuation, if the shared blocks were evicted, or if the request is routed elsewhere, the hit can be much smaller. Jumping to Z may preserve only the system prompt and initial tool definitions even though it starts from A. The precise cache management behavior here depends greatly on the providers.

sentences.en: If the cache keeps reusable prefix blocks, jumping from D to F may still reuse root -> C.
sentences.zh: 如果缓存保留可重用的前缀块，从 D 跳转到 F 仍可能重用 root -> C。

sentences.en: If it only retains the hottest continuation, if the shared blocks were evicted, or if the request is routed elsewhere, the hit can be much smaller.
sentences.zh: 如果它只保留最热的延续，如果共享块被驱逐，或者如果请求被路由到其他地方，那么命中可能会小得多。

sentences.en: Jumping to Z may preserve only the system prompt and initial tool definitions even though it starts from A.
sentences.zh: 跳转到 Z 可能只保留系统提示符和初始工具定义，即使它是从 A 开始的。

sentences.en: The precise cache management behavior here depends greatly on the providers.
sentences.zh: 这里精确的缓存管理行为很大程度上取决于提供者。

blocks.html_en: The reverse can also happen. /fork or a new session can produce a new session ID while carrying over a large amount of identical context. A routing system that isolates caches by session key may fail to notice that useful overlap.

sentences.en: The reverse can also happen. /fork or a new session can produce a new session ID while carrying over a large amount of identical context.
sentences.zh: 相反的情况也可能发生。 /fork 或新会话可以产生新的会话 ID，同时承载大量相同的上下文。

sentences.en: A routing system that isolates caches by session key may fail to notice that useful overlap.
sentences.zh: 通过会话密钥隔离缓存的路由系统可能无法注意到有用的重叠。

blocks.html_en: The reusable prefix determines what work can be cached. Session identity merely helps the infrastructure find likely content. On some systems the routing key is crucial to manage caches, on others it's merely an optimization.

sentences.en: The reusable prefix determines what work can be cached.
sentences.zh: 可重用前缀决定了可以缓存哪些工作。

sentences.en: Session identity merely helps the infrastructure find likely content.
sentences.zh: 会话身份仅仅帮助基础设施找到可能的内容。

sentences.en: On some systems the routing key is crucial to manage caches, on others it's merely an optimization.
sentences.zh: 在某些系统上，路由密钥对于管理缓存至关重要，而在其他系统上，它只是一种优化。

blocks.html_en: Explicit vs Automatic Prefix Caching

sentences.en: Explicit vs Automatic Prefix Caching
sentences.zh: 显式前缀缓存与自动前缀缓存

blocks.html_en: Provider APIs expose caching in two main styles.

sentences.en: Provider APIs expose caching in two main styles.
sentences.zh: 提供者 API 以两种主要方式公开缓存。

blocks.html_en: Anthropic's traditional interface uses explicit cache_control points. The client marks boundaries after stable parts of the request, such as the system prompt, tool definitions, or the latest cacheable conversation content. The server can then write or look up the prefix ending at those points. The boundary is explicit, but reuse still requires the content before it to match. Not only are the cache points explicit, so is the pricing. You pay for cache writes, and you get to choose for how long which comes at different price points.

sentences.en: Anthropic's traditional interface uses explicit cache_control points.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 的传统接口使用显式的缓存控制点。

sentences.en: The client marks boundaries after stable parts of the request, such as the system prompt, tool definitions, or the latest cacheable conversation content.
sentences.zh: 客户端在请求的稳定部分（例如系统提示、工具定义或最新的可缓存对话内容）之后标记边界。

sentences.en: The server can then write or look up the prefix ending at those points.
sentences.zh: 然后，服务器可以写入或查找以这些点结尾的前缀。

sentences.en: The boundary is explicit, but reuse still requires the content before it to match.
sentences.zh: 边界是明确的，但重用仍然需要其之前的内容匹配。

sentences.en: Not only are the cache points explicit, so is the pricing.
sentences.zh: 不仅缓存点是明确的，定价也是如此。

sentences.en: You pay for cache writes, and you get to choose for how long which comes at different price points.
sentences.zh: 您需要为缓存写入付费，并且可以选择不同价格点的持续时间。

blocks.html_en: Other APIs use automatic prefix caching. The client sends the request normally, and the provider finds a reusable prefix without client-placed breakpoints. A prompt-cache key or session header may improve routing or grouping, but it does not make different prefixes equal.

sentences.en: Other APIs use automatic prefix caching.
sentences.zh: 其他 API 使用自动前缀缓存。

sentences.en: The client sends the request normally, and the provider finds a reusable prefix without client-placed breakpoints.
sentences.zh: 客户端正常发送请求，提供者找到可重用的前缀，而无需客户端放置断点。

sentences.en: A prompt-cache key or session header may improve routing or grouping, but it does not make different prefixes equal.
sentences.zh: 提示缓存键或会话标头可以改进路由或分组，但它不会使不同的前缀相等。

blocks.html_en: Why Tool Loadouts Trash Caches

sentences.en: Why Tool Loadouts Trash Caches
sentences.zh: 为什么工具加载会成为垃圾缓存

blocks.html_en: Tool definitions usually appear before the conversation and they are "folded" into the system prompt internally. Their names, descriptions, and JSON schemas are model input just like any other text. Adding one tool, removing one, changing its schema, or even serializing the tools in a different order can move the first mismatch close to the start of the prompt.

sentences.en: Tool definitions usually appear before the conversation and they are "folded" into the system prompt internally.
sentences.zh: 工具定义通常出现在对话之前，并且它们在内部“折叠”到系统提示符中。

sentences.en: Their names, descriptions, and JSON schemas are model input just like any other text.
sentences.zh: 它们的名称、描述和 JSON 模式是模型输入，就像任何其他文本一样。

sentences.en: Adding one tool, removing one, changing its schema, or even serializing the tools in a different order can move the first mismatch close to the start of the prompt.
sentences.zh: 添加一个工具、删除一个工具、更改其架构，甚至以不同的顺序序列化工具，都可以将第一个不匹配移动到提示符开头附近。

blocks.html_en: This is a common surprise with plugin systems and MCP-style tool catalogs. Loading a tool only when it becomes relevant sounds efficient because fewer schemas are sent initially. On most models, however, the newly expanded loadout invalidates the cached conversation that follows it. Saving a few tool-schema tokens can cause tens of thousands of conversation tokens to be processed again.

sentences.en: This is a common surprise with plugin systems and MCP-style tool catalogs.
sentences.zh: 这是插件系统和 MCP 风格的工具目录中常见的惊喜。

sentences.en: Loading a tool only when it becomes relevant sounds efficient because fewer schemas are sent initially.
sentences.zh: 仅当工具变得相关时才加载工具听起来很高效，因为最初发送的模式较少。

sentences.en: On most models, however, the newly expanded loadout invalidates the cached conversation that follows it.
sentences.zh: 然而，在大多数模型上，新扩展的加载会使后面的缓存对话无效。

sentences.en: Saving a few tool-schema tokens can cause tens of thousands of conversation tokens to be processed again.
sentences.zh: 保存一些工具模式令牌可能会导致再次处理数以万计的对话令牌。

blocks.html_en: Some newer model APIs support additive tool loading. A tool can become available at a specific tool result inside the transcript instead of being inserted into the original tool list. The old prefix remains unchanged:

sentences.en: Some newer model APIs support additive tool loading.
sentences.zh: 一些较新的模型 API 支持附加工具加载。

sentences.en: A tool can become available at a specific tool result inside the transcript instead of being inserted into the original tool list.
sentences.zh: 工具可以在转录本内的特定工具结果处变得可用，而不是插入到原始工具列表中。

sentences.en: The old prefix remains unchanged:
sentences.zh: 旧前缀保持不变：

blocks.html_en: Pi nowadays supports this for models with native deferred-tool mechanisms. When an extension makes a purely additive change with setActiveTools(), Pi records the added names on the tool result. For supported Anthropic models it uses deferred definitions and a tool_reference and for supported OpenAI models it emits the corresponding tool-search items. Other models get a safe fallback: Pi sends the complete active tool list on the next request, which works functionally but may wipe the prompt cache.

sentences.en: Pi nowadays supports this for models with native deferred-tool mechanisms.
sentences.zh: Pi 现在支持具有本机延迟工具机制的模型。

sentences.en: When an extension makes a purely additive change with setActiveTools(), Pi records the added names on the tool result.
sentences.zh: 当扩展使用 setActiveTools() 进行纯粹的附加更改时，Pi 会在工具结果中记录添加的名称。

sentences.en: For supported Anthropic models it uses deferred definitions and a tool_reference and for supported OpenAI models it emits the corresponding tool-search items.
sentences.zh: 对于支持的 Anthropic 模型，它使用延迟定义和 tool_reference，对于支持的 OpenAI 模型，它会发出相应的工具搜索项。

sentences.en: Other models get a safe fallback: Pi sends the complete active tool list on the next request, which works functionally but may wipe the prompt cache.
sentences.zh: 其他模型获得安全后备：Pi 在下一个请求时发送完整的活动工具列表，该列表可以正常工作，但可能会擦除提示缓存。

blocks.html_en: The word additive matters as removing tools, replacing one loadout with another, or changing prompt snippets still changes earlier input. An extension that rebuilds the system prompt, shuffles tool order, injects timestamps, or changes active tools every turn can accidentally defeat caching for the entire session.

sentences.en: The word additive matters as removing tools, replacing one loadout with another, or changing prompt snippets still changes earlier input.
sentences.zh: “添加剂”一词很重要，因为删除工具、用另一种负载替换一种负载或更改提示片段仍然会更改早期的输入。

sentences.en: An extension that rebuilds the system prompt, shuffles tool order, injects timestamps, or changes active tools every turn can accidentally defeat caching for the entire session.
sentences.zh: 重建系统提示、打乱工具顺序、注入时间戳或每次更改活动工具的扩展可能会意外地破坏整个会话的缓存。

blocks.html_en: Extensibility means Pi cannot guarantee cache stability on behalf of every extension. We can provide cache-friendly mechanisms; extensions still have to use them and from what we have seen, for many extensions cache efficiency is an afterthought. This is, in part, because when you pay on a fixed subscription the associated cost with cache misses is not quite as obvious.

sentences.en: Extensibility means Pi cannot guarantee cache stability on behalf of every extension.
sentences.zh: 可扩展性意味着 Pi 无法代表每个扩展保证缓存稳定性。

sentences.en: We can provide cache-friendly mechanisms; extensions still have to use them and from what we have seen, for many extensions cache efficiency is an afterthought.
sentences.zh: 我们可以提供缓存友好的机制；扩展仍然必须使用它们，从我们所看到的来看，对于许多扩展来说，缓存效率是事后才想到的。

sentences.en: This is, in part, because when you pay on a fixed subscription the associated cost with cache misses is not quite as obvious.
sentences.zh: 部分原因是，当您支付固定订阅费用时，与缓存未命中相关的成本并不那么明显。

blocks.html_en: Interruptions and TTLs

sentences.en: Interruptions and TTLs
sentences.zh: 中断和 TTL

blocks.html_en: Some important prompt caches have short default lifetimes. Anthropic's default five-minute cache is particularly important because it is shorter than many normal coding activities. If you go to sip a coffee when using Fable, and you come back 10 minutes later, a single "say hi" message will cost you more money than you expect.

sentences.en: Some important prompt caches have short default lifetimes.
sentences.zh: 一些重要的提示缓存的默认生存期很短。

sentences.en: Anthropic's default five-minute cache is particularly important because it is shorter than many normal coding activities.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 默认的五分钟缓存特别重要，因为它比许多正常的编码活动要短。

sentences.en: If you go to sip a coffee when using Fable, and you come back 10 minutes later, a single "say hi" message will cost you more money than you expect.
sentences.zh: 如果您在使用《神鬼寓言》时去喝咖啡，10 分钟后回来，一条“打招呼”消息将花费您比您预期更多的钱。

blocks.html_en: That's because while the user may think of a coding session as continuously active, the inference provider sees a sequence of isolated requests:

sentences.en: That's because while the user may think of a coding session as continuously active, the inference provider sees a sequence of isolated requests:
sentences.zh: 这是因为，虽然用户可能认为编码会话是持续活动的，但推理提供者看到的是一系列孤立的请求：

blocks.html_en: A long build, a test suite, lunch, a meeting, or simply stopping to review a diff can outlive the cache. The next request contains the same prompt, but the stored KV state is gone and the prefix is billed again as input.

sentences.en: A long build, a test suite, lunch, a meeting, or simply stopping to review a diff can outlive the cache.
sentences.zh: 长时间的构建、测试套件、午餐、会议或只是停下来检查差异可能会比缓存的寿命更长。

sentences.en: The next request contains the same prompt, but the stored KV state is gone and the prefix is billed again as input.
sentences.zh: 下一个请求包含相同的提示，但存储的 KV 状态消失了，并且前缀作为输入再次计费。

blocks.html_en: Since Pi is currently not a permitted harness on Anthropic's subscription we're following the 5 minute default that Anthropic recommends for API users. However from looking at Claude Code's codebase we know that for their own subscription users, they are increasing that cache timeout to one hour. The increased cost of this however often is not worth it, when you need to pay API token prices.

sentences.en: Since Pi is currently not a permitted harness on Anthropic's subscription we're following the 5 minute default that Anthropic recommends for API users.
sentences.zh: 由于 Pi 目前不是 Anthropic 订阅中允许的工具，因此我们遵循 Anthropic 为 API 用户推荐的 5 分钟默认设置。

sentences.en: However from looking at Claude Code's codebase we know that for their own subscription users, they are increasing that cache timeout to one hour.
sentences.zh: 然而，通过查看 Claude Code 的代码库，我们知道对于他们自己的订阅用户，他们将缓存超时增加到一小时。

sentences.en: The increased cost of this however often is not worth it, when you need to pay API token prices.
sentences.zh: 然而，当您需要支付 API 令牌价格时，这种增加的成本通常是不值得的。

blocks.html_en: But you can opt into this. Some providers such as Anthropic expose longer retention controls. For supported direct APIs, Pi users can set PI_CACHE_RETENTION=long to request them. That is still only a request: Pi cannot force a gateway to retain an entry, prevent eviction under memory pressure, or keep a cache alive while no model request is being made.

sentences.en: But you can opt into this.
sentences.zh: 但您可以选择这样做。

sentences.en: Some providers such as Anthropic expose longer retention controls.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 等一些提供商公开了更长的保留控制。

sentences.en: For supported direct APIs, Pi users can set PI_CACHE_RETENTION=long to request them.
sentences.zh: 对于支持的直接 API，Pi 用户可以设置 PI_CACHE_RETENTION=long 来请求它们。

sentences.en: That is still only a request: Pi cannot force a gateway to retain an entry, prevent eviction under memory pressure, or keep a cache alive while no model request is being made.
sentences.zh: 这仍然只是一个请求：Pi 无法强制网关保留条目、防止在内存压力下被驱逐，或者在没有发出模型请求时保持缓存处于活动状态。

blocks.html_en: The Price of a Miss

sentences.en: The Price of a Miss
sentences.zh: 小姐的代价

blocks.html_en: Providers usually price uncached input, cache writes, and cache reads differently. Cache reads are commonly discounted because the expensive prefill work has already happened. Cache writes can carry a premium because the provider is promising to retain state for later use.

sentences.en: Providers usually price uncached input, cache writes, and cache reads differently.
sentences.zh: 提供商通常对未缓存的输入、缓存写入和缓存读取进行不同的定价。

sentences.en: Cache reads are commonly discounted because the expensive prefill work has already happened.
sentences.zh: 缓存读取通常会打折扣，因为昂贵的预填充工作已经发生。

sentences.en: Cache writes can carry a premium because the provider is promising to retain state for later use.
sentences.zh: 缓存写入可能会带来额外费用，因为提供商承诺保留状态以供以后使用。

blocks.html_en: Imagine a coding session with 100,000 tokens of history followed by a short new request as the Fable example above. When the cache works, almost all of that history is charged at the lower cache-read price. Only the small amount of new material needs to be processed at the regular input price and potentially written to the cache.

sentences.en: Imagine a coding session with 100,000 tokens of history followed by a short new request as the Fable example above.
sentences.zh: 想象一下具有 100,000 个历史标记的编码会话，然后是一个简短的新请求，如上面的 Fable 示例。

sentences.en: When the cache works, almost all of that history is charged at the lower cache-read price.
sentences.zh: 当缓存工作时，几乎所有历史记录都按较低的缓存读取价格收费。

sentences.en: Only the small amount of new material needs to be processed at the regular input price and potentially written to the cache.
sentences.zh: 只有少量的新材料需要以常规输入价格进行处理并可能写入缓存。

blocks.html_en: When the cache misses, the provider has to process the entire 100,000-token history again at the regular input price. It may also charge to write that history back into the cache. This is why a short request such as continue can be surprisingly expensive after a cache expires. In a long coding session, re-reading the old input can cost much more than generating the next answer.

sentences.en: When the cache misses, the provider has to process the entire 100,000-token history again at the regular input price.
sentences.zh: 当缓存未命中时，提供者必须以常规输入价格再次处理整个 100,000 个代币历史记录。

sentences.en: It may also charge to write that history back into the cache.
sentences.zh: 它还可能需要将该历史记录写回到缓存中。

sentences.en: This is why a short request such as continue can be surprisingly expensive after a cache expires.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么在缓存过期后，诸如 continue 之类的短请求可能会非常昂贵。

sentences.en: In a long coding session, re-reading the old input can cost much more than generating the next answer.
sentences.zh: 在长时间的编码会话中，重新读取旧输入的成本比生成下一个答案的成本要高得多。

blocks.html_en: Caching also has a chance to create non-obvious incentives.

sentences.en: Caching also has a chance to create non-obvious incentives.
sentences.zh: 缓存还有机会产生不明显的激励。

blocks.html_en: The user should want high hit rates because they reduce latency and price. An inference operator that owns the GPUs should want them too: less prefill work means more requests served with the same hardware. A well-designed cached-token discount can align both sides while leaving the operator with better margins.

sentences.en: The user should want high hit rates because they reduce latency and price.
sentences.zh: 用户应该想要高命中率，因为它们可以减少延迟和价格。

sentences.en: An inference operator that owns the GPUs should want them too: less prefill work means more requests served with the same hardware.
sentences.zh: 拥有 GPU 的推理运算符也应该需要它们：更少的预填充工作意味着使用相同的硬件处理更多的请求。

sentences.en: A well-designed cached-token discount can align both sides while leaving the operator with better margins.
sentences.zh: 精心设计的缓存代币折扣可以使双方保持一致，同时为运营商带来更好的利润。

blocks.html_en: A gateway or reseller can have a different incentive. If it earns revenue from input tokens billed at the uncached rate, a cache miss can produce a larger customer invoice than a hit. Whether that also produces more profit depends on its upstream costs, contracts, and who operates the cache. In a badly aligned stack, the party responsible for routing may not bear the full cost of misses, while the party billing the user earns more revenue when they happen.

sentences.en: A gateway or reseller can have a different incentive.
sentences.zh: 网关或经销商可能有不同的激励措施。

sentences.en: If it earns revenue from input tokens billed at the uncached rate, a cache miss can produce a larger customer invoice than a hit.
sentences.zh: 如果它通过以未缓存费率计费的输入令牌赚取收入，则缓存未命中可能会产生比命中更大的客户发票。

sentences.en: Whether that also produces more profit depends on its upstream costs, contracts, and who operates the cache.
sentences.zh: 这是否也能产生更多利润取决于其上游成本、合同以及谁运营缓存。

sentences.en: In a badly aligned stack, the party responsible for routing may not bear the full cost of misses, while the party billing the user earns more revenue when they happen.
sentences.zh: 在对齐不良的堆栈中，负责路由的一方可能无法承担未命中的全部成本，而向用户计费的一方在发生错误时会获得更多收入。

blocks.html_en: That does not mean providers sabotage caches but it means cache performance should be observable. Users should not have to infer that only from a surprisingly large bill. Understanding if something odd is going on with caches can be an important insight.

sentences.en: That does not mean providers sabotage caches but it means cache performance should be observable.
sentences.zh: 这并不意味着提供商破坏缓存，而是意味着缓存性能应该是可观察的。

sentences.en: Users should not have to infer that only from a surprisingly large bill.
sentences.zh: 用户不应该仅从大得惊人的账单中推断出这一点。

sentences.en: Understanding if something odd is going on with caches can be an important insight.
sentences.zh: 了解缓存是否发生异常可能是一个重要的见解。

blocks.html_en: Strict cache adherence also means less flexibility for a gateway to route you to the best option in-between turns. You might want to take a cache hit to continue with a different model which from that point onwards might be more economical, or it might be the case that you might be better off load balancing to another provider.

sentences.en: Strict cache adherence also means less flexibility for a gateway to route you to the best option in-between turns.
sentences.zh: 严格的缓存遵守还意味着网关在轮流之间将您路由到最佳选项的灵活性较低。

sentences.en: You might want to take a cache hit to continue with a different model which from that point onwards might be more economical, or it might be the case that you might be better off load balancing to another provider.
sentences.zh: 您可能希望通过缓存命中来继续使用不同的模型，从那时起，这可能会更经济，或者您可能会更好地将负载平衡转移给另一个提供商。

blocks.html_en: Why Pi Does Not Prune Aggressively

sentences.en: Why Pi Does Not Prune Aggressively
sentences.zh: 为什么 Pi 不积极修剪

blocks.html_en: Now that you've made it this far, you probably have an idea why Pi does not prune tool calls. It is tempting to control cost by continuously deleting old tool results or rewriting history and sometimes that is necessary, especially near the context-window limit. But as we have learned, pruning has a cache cost of its own.

sentences.en: Now that you've made it this far, you probably have an idea why Pi does not prune tool calls.
sentences.zh: 既然您已经做到了这一点，您可能已经知道为什么 Pi 不修剪工具调用了。

sentences.en: It is tempting to control cost by continuously deleting old tool results or rewriting history and sometimes that is necessary, especially near the context-window limit.
sentences.zh: 通过不断删除旧的工具结果或重写历史记录来控制成本是很诱人的，有时这是必要的，特别是在上下文窗口限制附近。

sentences.en: But as we have learned, pruning has a cache cost of its own.
sentences.zh: 但正如我们所知，修剪有其自身的缓存成本。

blocks.html_en: Deleting content from the middle changes the prefix at the deletion point. All surviving conversation after it may need to be processed again. The immediate cost of rewriting a long cached context can exceed the future savings from removing a small number of cheaply cached tokens.

sentences.en: Deleting content from the middle changes the prefix at the deletion point.
sentences.zh: 从中间删除内容会更改删除点处的前缀。

sentences.en: All surviving conversation after it may need to be processed again.
sentences.zh: 之后所有幸存的对话可能需要再次处理。

sentences.en: The immediate cost of rewriting a long cached context can exceed the future savings from removing a small number of cheaply cached tokens.
sentences.zh: 重写长缓存上下文的直接成本可能超过删除少量廉价缓存令牌所节省的未来成本。

blocks.html_en: A rough break-even comparison is:

sentences.en: A rough break-even comparison is:
sentences.zh: 粗略的收支平衡比较是：

blocks.html_en: This is not only an accounting question as old tool results often contain the evidence the model used to make later decisions. Removing them can degrade behavior even if a summary preserves the gist.

sentences.en: This is not only an accounting question as old tool results often contain the evidence the model used to make later decisions.
sentences.zh: 这不仅仅是一个会计问题，因为旧工具的结果通常包含模型用于做出后续决策的证据。

sentences.en: Removing them can degrade behavior even if a summary preserves the gist.
sentences.zh: 即使摘要保留了要点，删除它们也会降低行为。

blocks.html_en: Pi therefore prefers a stable, append-oriented transcript and does not treat every old token as waste. Compaction is available when context pressure justifies a lossy rewrite. Because compaction deliberately creates new context rather than accidentally re-billing an unchanged prompt, Pi treats it as a cache reset rather than a cache failure in its session statistics.

sentences.en: Pi therefore prefers a stable, append-oriented transcript and does not treat every old token as waste.
sentences.zh: 因此，Pi 更喜欢稳定的、面向附加的转录本，并且不会将每个旧令牌视为浪费。

sentences.en: Compaction is available when context pressure justifies a lossy rewrite.
sentences.zh: 当上下文压力证明有损重写合理时，可以使用压缩。

sentences.en: Because compaction deliberately creates new context rather than accidentally re-billing an unchanged prompt, Pi treats it as a cache reset rather than a cache failure in its session statistics.
sentences.zh: 由于压缩有意创建新的上下文，而不是意外地重新计费未更改的提示，因此 Pi 在其会话统计信息中将其视为缓存重置而不是缓存故障。

blocks.html_en: The goal is not the smallest possible prompt but the best trade-off among model context, cache reuse, latency, and price.

sentences.en: The goal is not the smallest possible prompt but the best trade-off among model context, cache reuse, latency, and price.
sentences.zh: 目标不是尽可能最小的提示，而是模型上下文、缓存重用、延迟和价格之间的最佳权衡。

blocks.html_en: Simultaneously there can be a case for pruning too. If you are working with providers that do not discount you for good cache usage, or it's for whatever reason not possible to get high cache rates, it might be preferable to prune. It definitely improves the opportunity for the router to balance between different backends as caches are not transferable.

sentences.en: Simultaneously there can be a case for pruning too.
sentences.zh: 同时也可能存在修剪的情况。

sentences.en: If you are working with providers that do not discount you for good cache usage, or it's for whatever reason not possible to get high cache rates, it might be preferable to prune.
sentences.zh: 如果您正在与提供商合作，但他们不会因为良好的缓存使用而打折扣，或者由于某种原因无法获得高缓存率，那么修剪可能会更好。

sentences.en: It definitely improves the opportunity for the router to balance between different backends as caches are not transferable.
sentences.zh: 由于缓存不可转移，这无疑提高了路由器在不同后端之间平衡的机会。

blocks.html_en: What Pi Can and Cannot Do

sentences.en: What Pi Can and Cannot Do
sentences.zh: Pi 能做什么和不能做什么

blocks.html_en: Pi works to keep stable inputs stable. It passes a consistent session ID and provider-specific cache hints, places explicit cache points for APIs that require them, records cache-read and cache-write usage, and supports message-anchored additive tool loading where models allow it. Its default transcript behavior also avoids gratuitously rewriting old context.

sentences.en: Pi works to keep stable inputs stable.
sentences.zh: Pi 致力于保持稳定的输入稳定。

sentences.en: It passes a consistent session ID and provider-specific cache hints, places explicit cache points for APIs that require them, records cache-read and cache-write usage, and supports message-anchored additive tool loading where models allow it.
sentences.zh: 它传递一致的会话 ID 和特定于提供者的缓存提示，为需要它们的 API 放置显式缓存点，记录缓存读取和缓存写入使用情况，并在模型允许的情况下支持消息锚定的附加工具加载。

sentences.en: Its default transcript behavior also avoids gratuitously rewriting old context.
sentences.zh: 它的默认转录行为也避免了无端重写旧上下文。

blocks.html_en: Pi cannot control every layer after the request leaves the machine. It cannot choose a provider's eviction policy, extend a cache beyond what the API permits, keep a particular GPU alive, or guarantee that a gateway honors affinity. It also cannot preserve a prefix that an extension changes.

sentences.en: Pi cannot control every layer after the request leaves the machine.
sentences.zh: 请求离开机器后，Pi 无法控制每一层。

sentences.en: It cannot choose a provider's eviction policy, extend a cache beyond what the API permits, keep a particular GPU alive, or guarantee that a gateway honors affinity.
sentences.zh: 它无法选择提供商的逐出策略、将缓存扩展到 API 允许的范围之外、保持特定 GPU 处于活动状态或保证网关尊重关联性。

sentences.en: It also cannot preserve a prefix that an extension changes.
sentences.zh: 它也无法保留扩展名更改的前缀。

blocks.html_en: What it can do is make cache health visible.

sentences.en: What it can do is make cache health visible.
sentences.zh: 它可以做的就是使缓存健康状况可见。

blocks.html_en: The interactive footer shows cumulative cache reads and writes as R and W, plus CH for the latest request's cache-hit rate. The /session command gives a fuller view: total cached and uncached input, cumulative hit rate, cost, and an estimate of tokens and dollars re-billed by significant cache misses.

sentences.en: The interactive footer shows cumulative cache reads and writes as R and W, plus CH for the latest request's cache-hit rate.
sentences.zh: 交互式页脚将累积缓存读取和写入显示为 R 和 W，以及最新请求的缓存命中率的 CH。

sentences.en: The /session command gives a fuller view: total cached and uncached input, cumulative hit rate, cost, and an estimate of tokens and dollars re-billed by significant cache misses.
sentences.zh: /session 命令提供了更全面的视图：缓存和未缓存的输入总数、累积命中率、成本以及因重大缓存未命中而重新计费的令牌和美元的估计。

blocks.html_en: Users who want misses called out as they happen can enable Show cache miss notices in /settings, corresponding to showCacheMissNotices in settings.json. Pi then inserts a warning after a significant miss, including the estimated re-billed tokens and cost. When it can observe a model switch or an idle gap beyond the usual short TTL, it says so. For other misses it reports the fact without pretending to know what happened inside the provider.

sentences.en: Users who want misses called out as they happen can enable Show cache miss notices in /settings, corresponding to showCacheMissNotices in settings.json.
sentences.zh: 如果用户希望在发生未命中事件时被告知，可以在 /settings 中启用“显示缓存未命中通知”，对应于 settings.json 中的 showCacheMissNotices。

sentences.en: Pi then inserts a warning after a significant miss, including the estimated re-billed tokens and cost.
sentences.zh: 然后，Pi 在重大失误后插入警告，包括估计的重新计费代币和成本。

sentences.en: When it can observe a model switch or an idle gap beyond the usual short TTL, it says so.
sentences.zh: 当它可以观察到模型切换或超出通常的短 TTL 的空闲间隙时，它会这样说。

sentences.en: For other misses it reports the fact without pretending to know what happened inside the provider.
sentences.zh: 对于其他未命中的情况，它会报告事实，但不会假装知道提供者内部发生了什么。

blocks.html_en: Common Reasons for Worse Cache Performance

sentences.en: Common Reasons for Worse Cache Performance
sentences.zh: 缓存性能较差的常见原因

blocks.html_en: When a session's cache-hit rate looks wrong, the usual causes are:

sentences.en: When a session's cache-hit rate looks wrong, the usual causes are:
sentences.zh: 当会话的缓存命中率看起来不正确时，通常的原因是：

blocks.html_en: • Idling. A command, review, or conversation pause exceeds the provider's retention window.

sentences.en: Idling.
sentences.zh: 空转。

sentences.en: A command, review, or conversation pause exceeds the provider's retention window.
sentences.zh: 命令、审阅或对话暂停超出了提供商的保留窗口。

blocks.html_en: • Model or provider switches. KV state is model-specific and generally does not move across providers.

sentences.en: Model or provider switches.
sentences.zh: 型号或提供商切换。

sentences.en: KV state is model-specific and generally does not move across providers.
sentences.zh: KV 状态是特定于模型的，通常不会跨提供商移动。

blocks.html_en: • Branch navigation. /tree, rewinds, forks, and alternate branches can change the active token sequence even when the session ID remains the same.

sentences.en: Branch navigation. /tree, rewinds, forks, and alternate branches can change the active token sequence even when the session ID remains the same.
sentences.zh: 分行导航。即使会话 ID 保持不变，/tree、倒带、分叉和备用分支也可以更改活动令牌序列。

blocks.html_en: • Compaction or manual history rewriting. These intentionally replace part of the prompt and establish a new prefix.

sentences.en: Compaction or manual history rewriting.
sentences.zh: 压缩或手动历史记录重写。

sentences.en: These intentionally replace part of the prompt and establish a new prefix.
sentences.zh: 这些有意替换部分提示并建立新的前缀。

blocks.html_en: • Tool and reasoning level changes. Adding, removing, reordering, or editing tool definitions changes an early part of the request unless the model supports message-anchored loading and the change is purely additive. Reasoning level changes usually have the same effect.

sentences.en: Tool and reasoning level changes.
sentences.zh: 工具和推理水平发生变化。

sentences.en: Adding, removing, reordering, or editing tool definitions changes an early part of the request unless the model supports message-anchored loading and the change is purely additive.
sentences.zh: 添加、删除、重新排序或编辑工具定义会更改请求的早期部分，除非模型支持消息锚定加载并且更改纯粹是附加的。

sentences.en: Reasoning level changes usually have the same effect.
sentences.zh: 推理水平的改变通常会产生相同的效果。

blocks.html_en: • Dynamic system prompts. Timestamps, random values, changing project context, and extension-provided prompt snippets can invalidate everything after them.

sentences.en: Dynamic system prompts.
sentences.zh: 动态系统提示。

sentences.en: Timestamps, random values, changing project context, and extension-provided prompt snippets can invalidate everything after them.
sentences.zh: 时间戳、随机值、更改项目上下文和扩展提供的提示片段可能会使它们之后的所有内容无效。

blocks.html_en: • Extension context transforms. An extension that modifies old messages or provider payloads can make an apparently stable Pi transcript unstable on the wire.

sentences.en: Extension context transforms.
sentences.zh: 扩展上下文变换。

sentences.en: An extension that modifies old messages or provider payloads can make an apparently stable Pi transcript unstable on the wire.
sentences.zh: 修改旧消息或提供程序有效负载的扩展可能会使看似稳定的 Pi 转录本在网络上变得不稳定。

blocks.html_en: • Provider routing and eviction. The prompt can be identical and still miss because the relevant KV blocks are no longer available where the request lands.

sentences.en: Provider routing and eviction.
sentences.zh: 提供商路由和驱逐。

sentences.en: The prompt can be identical and still miss because the relevant KV blocks are no longer available where the request lands.
sentences.zh: 提示可能是相同的，但仍然会丢失，因为相关的 KV 块在请求到达的地方不再可用。

content_html_zh:
大型语言模型通常被认为是类似的函数：发送一些文本，接收一些文本。 这是一个有用的抽象，但它忽略了运行编码代理的最重要部分之一：大部分输入与上次相同。 换句话说，我们主要是附加到它上面。

编码代理向模型发送其系统提示、工具定义、项目说明、对话历史记录、工具调用和工具结果。 在下一个回合中，它会再次发送几乎所有内容，以及少量新材料。 一旦会话增长到数万或数十万个令牌，为每个回合重新计算整个提示既缓慢又昂贵。

即时缓存使得这有点经济，但它也相当脆弱。 更改的工具定义、模型切换或提供者路由决策可以将人们期望的廉价增量请求转变为上下文的完整重播。

因此，对于编码代理来说，缓存行为不仅仅是实现细节或优化。 它会影响延迟、成本、工具设计、会话设计，甚至应该提供哪些产品功能。

KV缓存包含什么

变压器分两个主要阶段处理提示。 在预填充期间，它读取输入标记并计算它们的注意力状态。 在解码过程中，它一次产生一个新的令牌。

在每个注意力层，每个处理的令牌都会生成一个键和一个值。 这些与哈希表中的键值查找不太相似：两者都是数字数组，通常是浮点数或较低精度的量化值。 处理新令牌时，模型会将该令牌的查询与早期的键进行比较，以确定每个早期令牌的相关程度。 然后，它使用这些相关性分数来形成相应值的加权混合。 从这个意义上说，键是模型匹配的内容，而值是它检索的信息（但查找是模糊的，而不是像字典查找那样“返回单个精确匹配”。）

这些键和值将被保留，以便下一个生成的令牌可以处理之前发生的所有事情，而无需重新计算早期的令牌。 这个保留的状态就是KV缓存。

从概念上讲，请求如下所示：

request 1:

[system][tools][user][assistant][tool result][user]
<--------------------- prefill -------------------->
                       |
                       K and V tensors per token and layer

request 2:

[system][tools][user][assistant][tool result][user][new]
<---------------- reusable prefix ----------------><--->
                                                    |
                                                    new work

真实的表示更加复杂，特定于模型，并且“相当”大。 重要的属性是它们对应于特定的令牌前缀。 两个含义相同但标记不同的提示不共享 KV 缓存。 如果令牌在中间发生变化，则该令牌之后的所有内容都是不同的延续。

即时缓存将这种状态的生命周期延长了一代以上。 当来自编码代理的下一个 API 请求以相同的标记开始时，推理系统可以重用匹配前缀的存储工作，并仅预填充新的后缀。 到目前为止，理论。

缓存所在的位置

为了使缓存发挥作用，它需要存储在某个地方，并且需要可寻址。 推理系统有两种主要方式使 KV 缓存可供以后的请求使用。

更简单的方法是会话关联性。 它的工作原理是将 KV 缓存保留在计算它的 GPU 上或附近，并将下一个请求路由回同一个工作线程。 会话 ID 或提示缓存键成为一个简单的路由提示，因此您甚至可以在 HTTP 负载均衡器级别上处理此问题，而无需查看有效负载。

request(session-42) --> router --> worker 7 --> GPU 7 KV cache
next(session-42)    --> router --> worker 7 --> GPU 7 KV cache

这避免了通过网络移动非常大的缓存。 它工作时速度很快，但它限制了调度。 选定的工作线程可能会过载、重新启动或驱逐该条目。 路由器还可能认为平衡队列比保留一个会话的缓存更重要。 然而，它是一个非常有吸引力的解决方案，因为它几乎不需要额外部署的基础设施和硬件。

另一种方法是分布式缓存。 KV 块可以存储在另一内存层中，也可以跨工作线程使用，因此请求不会与一个 GPU 紧密联系。

                         +--------------------+
request --> scheduler -->| worker 3 / GPU 3   |
             |           +--------------------+
             |
             +----------> distributed KV blocks
             |
             +----------> worker 9 / GPU 9

这提高了调度灵活性和恢复能力，但是移动、索引和保留 KV 块本身就是一个系统问题。 实现以不同方式混合 GPU 内存、主机内存、本地存储、远程存储、前缀感知路由和逐出策略。

从角度来看 KV 缓存：它们可能很大，但在某些方面比人们想象的要小。 通过各种技巧，KV 缓存的大小可以减少到几 GB，即使对于长时间的对话也是如此。

缓存和前缀

Pi 会话是树，而不是列表。 /tree 可以将活动对话移回较早的点并沿着另一个分支继续。 倒回可以丢弃活动后缀，而不将其从会话文件中删除。 新分支可以共享大部分旧上下文，也可以共享一小部分，或者实际上完全不共享。 这种设计并不是 Pi 独有的，很多编码代理至少在概念上都有类似的东西。 即使您不将会话表示为树，代理进行某种形式的倒带也并不罕见。

                             +-- E -- F  another branch
                             |
session S: root -- A -- B -- C -- D  current branch
                   |
                   +-- Z  branch near the start

所有三个分支可以具有相同的 Pi 会话 ID。 从路由器的角度来看，它们是一个会话。 从提示缓存的角度来看，它们是三个标记序列，仅部分前缀重叠。

如果缓存保留可重用的前缀块，从 D 跳转到 F 仍可能重用 root -> C。 如果它只保留最热的延续，如果共享块被驱逐，或者如果请求被路由到其他地方，那么命中可能会小得多。 跳转到 Z 可能只保留系统提示符和初始工具定义，即使它是从 A 开始的。 这里精确的缓存管理行为很大程度上取决于提供者。

相反的情况也可能发生。 /fork 或新会话可以产生新的会话 ID，同时承载大量相同的上下文。 通过会话密钥隔离缓存的路由系统可能无法注意到有用的重叠。

可重用前缀决定了可以缓存哪些工作。 会话身份仅仅帮助基础设施找到可能的内容。 在某些系统上，路由密钥对于管理缓存至关重要，而在其他系统上，它只是一种优化。

显式前缀缓存与自动前缀缓存

提供者 API 以两种主要方式公开缓存。

Anthropic 的传统接口使用显式的缓存控制点。 客户端在请求的稳定部分（例如系统提示、工具定义或最新的可缓存对话内容）之后标记边界。 然后，服务器可以写入或查找以这些点结尾的前缀。 边界是明确的，但重用仍然需要其之前的内容匹配。 不仅缓存点是明确的，定价也是如此。 您需要为缓存写入付费，并且可以选择不同价格点的持续时间。

其他 API 使用自动前缀缓存。 客户端正常发送请求，提供者找到可重用的前缀，而无需客户端放置断点。 提示缓存键或会话标头可以改进路由或分组，但它不会使不同的前缀相等。

为什么工具加载会成为垃圾缓存

工具定义通常出现在对话之前，并且它们在内部“折叠”到系统提示符中。 它们的名称、描述和 JSON 模式是模型输入，就像任何其他文本一样。 添加一个工具、删除一个工具、更改其架构，甚至以不同的顺序序列化工具，都可以将第一个不匹配移动到提示符开头附近。

turn 1: [system][read][write][bash][conversation...........]
turn 2: [system][read][write][bash][deploy][conversation...]
                                   |
                                   old conversation is now
                                   after a mismatch

这是插件系统和 MCP 风格的工具目录中常见的惊喜。 仅当工具变得相关时才加载工具听起来很高效，因为最初发送的模式较少。 然而，在大多数模型上，新扩展的加载会使后面的缓存对话无效。 保存一些工具模式令牌可能会导致再次处理数以万计的对话令牌。

一些较新的模型 API 支持附加工具加载。 工具可以在转录本内的特定工具结果处变得可用，而不是插入到原始工具列表中。 旧前缀保持不变：

[system][initial tools][conversation][new tool][next turn]
<--------- cached prefix ----------->

Pi 现在支持具有本机延迟工具机制的模型。 当扩展使用 setActiveTools() 进行纯粹的附加更改时，Pi 会在工具结果中记录添加的名称。 对于支持的 Anthropic 模型，它使用延迟定义和 tool_reference，对于支持的 OpenAI 模型，它会发出相应的工具搜索项。 其他模型获得安全后备：Pi 在下一个请求时发送完整的活动工具列表，该列表可以正常工作，但可能会擦除提示缓存。

“添加剂”一词很重要，因为删除工具、用另一种负载替换一种负载或更改提示片段仍然会更改早期的输入。 重建系统提示、打乱工具顺序、注入时间戳或每次更改活动工具的扩展可能会意外地破坏整个会话的缓存。

可扩展性意味着 Pi 无法代表每个扩展保证缓存稳定性。 我们可以提供缓存友好的机制；扩展仍然必须使用它们，从我们所看到的来看，对于许多扩展来说，缓存效率是事后才想到的。 部分原因是，当您支付固定订阅费用时，与缓存未命中相关的成本并不那么明显。

中断和 TTL

一些重要的提示缓存的默认生存期很短。 Anthropic 默认的五分钟缓存特别重要，因为它比许多正常的编码活动要短。 如果您在使用《神鬼寓言》时去喝咖啡，10 分钟后回来，一条“打招呼”消息将花费您比您预期更多的钱。

这是因为，虽然用户可能认为编码会话是持续活动的，但推理提供者看到的是一系列孤立的请求：

model request --> run tests for 7 minutes --> model request
                  no cache traffic here

长时间的构建、测试套件、午餐、会议或只是停下来检查差异可能会比缓存的寿命更长。 下一个请求包含相同的提示，但存储的 KV 状态消失了，并且前缀作为输入再次计费。

由于 Pi 目前不是 Anthropic 订阅中允许的工具，因此我们遵循 Anthropic 为 API 用户推荐的 5 分钟默认设置。 然而，通过查看 Claude Code 的代码库，我们知道对于他们自己的订阅用户，他们将缓存超时增加到一小时。 然而，当您需要支付 API 令牌价格时，这种增加的成本通常是不值得的。

但您可以选择这样做。 Anthropic 等一些提供商公开了更长的保留控制。 对于支持的直接 API，Pi 用户可以设置 PI_CACHE_RETENTION=long 来请求它们。 这仍然只是一个请求：Pi 无法强制网关保留条目、防止在内存压力下被驱逐，或者在没有发出模型请求时保持缓存处于活动状态。

小姐的代价

提供商通常对未缓存的输入、缓存写入和缓存读取进行不同的定价。 缓存读取通常会打折扣，因为昂贵的预填充工作已经发生。 缓存写入可能会带来额外费用，因为提供商承诺保留状态以供以后使用。

想象一下具有 100,000 个历史标记的编码会话，然后是一个简短的新请求，如上面的 Fable 示例。 当缓存工作时，几乎所有历史记录都按较低的缓存读取价格收费。 只有少量的新材料需要以常规输入价格进行处理并可能写入缓存。

当缓存未命中时，提供者必须以常规输入价格再次处理整个 100,000 个代币历史记录。 它还可能需要将该历史记录写回到缓存中。 这就是为什么在缓存过期后，诸如 continue 之类的短请求可能会非常昂贵。 在长时间的编码会话中，重新读取旧输入的成本比生成下一个答案的成本要高得多。

缓存还有机会产生不明显的激励。

用户应该想要高命中率，因为它们可以减少延迟和价格。 拥有 GPU 的推理运算符也应该需要它们：更少的预填充工作意味着使用相同的硬件处理更多的请求。 精心设计的缓存代币折扣可以使双方保持一致，同时为运营商带来更好的利润。

网关或经销商可能有不同的激励措施。 如果它通过以未缓存费率计费的输入令牌赚取收入，则缓存未命中可能会产生比命中更大的客户发票。 这是否也能产生更多利润取决于其上游成本、合同以及谁运营缓存。 在对齐不良的堆栈中，负责路由的一方可能无法承担未命中的全部成本，而向用户计费的一方在发生错误时会获得更多收入。

这并不意味着提供商破坏缓存，而是意味着缓存性能应该是可观察的。 用户不应该仅从大得惊人的账单中推断出这一点。 了解缓存是否发生异常可能是一个重要的见解。

严格的缓存遵守还意味着网关在轮流之间将您路由到最佳选项的灵活性较低。 您可能希望通过缓存命中来继续使用不同的模型，从那时起，这可能会更经济，或者您可能会更好地将负载平衡转移给另一个提供商。

为什么 Pi 不积极修剪

既然您已经做到了这一点，您可能已经知道为什么 Pi 不修剪工具调用了。 通过不断删除旧的工具结果或重写历史记录来控制成本是很诱人的，有时这是必要的，特别是在上下文窗口限制附近。 但正如我们所知，修剪有其自身的缓存成本。

从中间删除内容会更改删除点处的前缀。 之后所有幸存的对话可能需要再次处理。 重写长缓存上下文的直接成本可能超过删除少量廉价缓存令牌所节省的未来成本。

粗略的收支平衡比较是：

one-time rewrite cost
    ~= surviving tokens after the edit * (uncached price - cache-read price)

future savings per turn
    ~= pruned tokens * cache-read price

这不仅仅是一个会计问题，因为旧工具的结果通常包含模型用于做出后续决策的证据。 即使摘要保留了要点，删除它们也会降低行为。

因此，Pi 更喜欢稳定的、面向附加的转录本，并且不会将每个旧令牌视为浪费。 当上下文压力证明有损重写合理时，可以使用压缩。 由于压缩有意创建新的上下文，而不是意外地重新计费未更改的提示，因此 Pi 在其会话统计信息中将其视为缓存重置而不是缓存故障。

目标不是尽可能最小的提示，而是模型上下文、缓存重用、延迟和价格之间的最佳权衡。

同时也可能存在修剪的情况。 如果您正在与提供商合作，但他们不会因为良好的缓存使用而打折扣，或者由于某种原因无法获得高缓存率，那么修剪可能会更好。 由于缓存不可转移，这无疑提高了路由器在不同后端之间平衡的机会。

Pi 能做什么和不能做什么

Pi 致力于保持稳定的输入稳定。 它传递一致的会话 ID 和特定于提供者的缓存提示，为需要它们的 API 放置显式缓存点，记录缓存读取和缓存写入使用情况，并在模型允许的情况下支持消息锚定的附加工具加载。 它的默认转录行为也避免了无端重写旧上下文。

请求离开机器后，Pi 无法控制每一层。 它无法选择提供商的逐出策略、将缓存扩展到 API 允许的范围之外、保持特定 GPU 处于活动状态或保证网关尊重关联性。 它也无法保留扩展名更改的前缀。

它可以做的就是使缓存健康状况可见。

交互式页脚将累积缓存读取和写入显示为 R 和 W，以及最新请求的缓存命中率的 CH。 /session 命令提供了更全面的视图：缓存和未缓存的输入总数、累积命中率、成本以及因重大缓存未命中而重新计费的令牌和美元的估计。

如果用户希望在发生未命中事件时被告知，可以在 /settings 中启用“显示缓存未命中通知”，对应于 settings.json 中的 showCacheMissNotices。 然后，Pi 在重大失误后插入警告，包括估计的重新计费代币和成本。 当它可以观察到模型切换或超出通常的短 TTL 的空闲间隙时，它会这样说。 对于其他未命中的情况，它会报告事实，但不会假装知道提供者内部发生了什么。

缓存性能较差的常见原因

当会话的缓存命中率看起来不正确时，通常的原因是：

  1. 空转。 命令、审阅或对话暂停超出了提供商的保留窗口。
  2. 型号或提供商切换。 KV 状态是特定于模型的，通常不会跨提供商移动。
  3. 分行导航。即使会话 ID 保持不变，/tree、倒带、分叉和备用分支也可以更改活动令牌序列。
  4. 压缩或手动历史记录重写。 这些有意替换部分提示并建立新的前缀。
  5. 工具和推理水平发生变化。 添加、删除、重新排序或编辑工具定义会更改请求的早期部分，除非模型支持消息锚定加载并且更改纯粹是附加的。 推理水平的改变通常会产生相同的效果。
  6. 动态系统提示。 时间戳、随机值、更改项目上下文和扩展提供的提示片段可能会使它们之后的所有内容无效。
  7. 扩展上下文变换。 修改旧消息或提供程序有效负载的扩展可能会使看似稳定的 Pi 转录本在网络上变得不稳定。
  8. 提供商路由和驱逐。 提示可能是相同的，但仍然会丢失，因为相关的 KV 块在请求到达的地方不再可用。
